note: Added the tech_category column (column B) — a single canonical technology family per sourcetype, drawn from the controlled vocabulary app, aws, azure, gcp, linux, network, security, windows. It identifies the index technology type a sourcetype's data belongs to (e.g., index suffixes such as *_linux / *_aws / *_security), enabling index-type-based CIM macro administration where new workloads of a given technology type are picked up automatically. Categories were derived from observed index_type-to-sourcetype pairings and refined by vendor/product nature; cloud-native sources follow their cloud (aws/azure/gcp), host OS sources follow the OS (linux/windows), network appliances/flow -> network, and host/identity/data security tooling -> security. Also fixed a data_models typo (Endpont.Services -> Endpoint.Services). Removed 3 environment-specific SSSD per-domain sourcetype entries (per-domain SSSD logs are named sssd_<domain> from a deployment's sssd.conf and map identically to the generic sssd sourcetype). Normalized data_models dataset paths: dropped the redundant root dataset from Model.All_<root>.<child> values (e.g. Compute_Inventory.All_Inventory.User -> Compute_Inventory.User, Performance.All_Performance.Network -> Performance.Network) and standardized time-sync entries on the valid nested path Performance.OS.Timesync. Added a space to the combined scope value (security,operational -> security, operational) to match the comma+space multivalue delimiter used by the data_models column. 848 rows.
